heading: Reduction of bycatch in eastern tropical Pacific Ocean
label: 962 Reduction of bycatch in eastern tropical Pacific Ocean
notes: Codification, which directed the addition of this section at the end of the Tuna Conventions Act, was executed by adding this section at the end of the Tuna Conventions Act of 1950, to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Section 7(c) of Pub. L. 105–42
Amendments2015— substituted “vessels” for “vessel” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 114–81

Effective DateSection effective upon certification by Secretary of Commerce that sufficient funding is available to complete first year of study required by and that study has commenced, and certification by Secretary of State to Congress that binding resolution of Inter-American Tropical Tuna Commission or other legally binding instrument establishing International Dolphin Conservation Program has been adopted and is in force, see , set out as an Effective Date of 1997 Amendment note under .section 1414a(a) of this titlesection 8 of Pub. L. 105–42section 1362 of this title

children.text: to require, to the maximum extent practicable, that sea turtles and other threatened species and endangered species are released alive;
to reduce, to the maximum extent practicable, the harvest of nontarget species;
to reduce, to the maximum extent practicable, the mortality of nontarget species; and
to reduce, to the maximum extent practicable, the mortality of juveniles of the target species.
